title: Those Were The Days

jokes.text: Come on, Uncle Phil. Vogue.
jokes.setup: Will is encouraging his stuffy uncle to do the trendy Madonna dance
jokes.punchline: The incongruity of dignified Uncle Phil voguing
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character-based humor using cultural clash between Will's street culture and Phil's stuffiness

jokes.text: Oh, no. Will, sweetie, you've been snapping everything in sight for days.
jokes.setup: Will's obsessive photography behavior
jokes.punchline: Vivian's exasperated reaction to his constant picture-taking
jokes.explanation: Mild setup humor establishing Will's photography obsession. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: I wanted to do something meaningful and artistic... but ain't nobody getting naked.
jokes.setup: Will explains his photography assignment with high-minded artistic goals
jokes.punchline: Reveals his real motivation was getting nude photos
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure with teen horniness as the reveal

jokes.text: Carlton, this is a photography assignment. Not a Prince video.
jokes.setup: Carlton is apparently posing dramatically for his photo
jokes.punchline: Will mocks Carlton's over-the-top posing style
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific joke about Carlton's tendency toward dramatic flair

jokes.text: She was too busy calling me a pathetic servant of the capitalist fat cats.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey describes how the visitor insulted him
jokes.punchline: The overly political description contrasts with Geoffrey's refined manner
jokes.explanation: Sets up Marge's radical character while playing against Geoffrey's upper-class sensibilities

jokes.text: Don't look like you be needing it now.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions Phil stole her Afro comb in '71
jokes.punchline: Points out Phil is now bald
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective baldness joke with good economy

jokes.text: I take it El was blindingly handsome.
jokes.setup: Marge says Will looks like her old boyfriend El (Eldridge Cleaver)
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically assumes this means he's handsome
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith confident/cocky character moment

jokes.text: Was he related to Wally and the Beav?
jokes.setup: Carlton hears 'Cleaver' name
jokes.punchline: Confuses radical Eldridge Cleaver with TV family the Cleavers
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific joke showing Carlton's sheltered upbringing and cultural disconnect

jokes.text: Man, that's unbelievable. Who the hell are they?
jokes.setup: Will acts amazed at hearing these famous activist names
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has no idea who they are
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Will's ignorance of history while trying to look cool

jokes.text: Okay, and I'm 'About To Bust A Gut-a.'
jokes.setup: Learning Phil and Vivian had African names Olifami and Adisimbol
jokes.punchline: Will creates his own mock-African name meaning he's laughing hard
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that captures Will's reaction and creates a quotable moment

jokes.text: Would that have been between breakfast and lunch, or lunch and dinner?
jokes.setup: Marge mentions they held hunger strikes
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically questions when they found time to hunger strike
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about the practicality of hunger strikes

jokes.text: I wasn't at Woodstock.
jokes.setup: Marge enthusiastically describes wild Woodstock adventures with Vivian
jokes.punchline: Vivian flatly states she wasn't even there
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that deflates Marge's fabricated story with minimal words

jokes.text: Those two weirdoes are your father and I, and that ugly little kid is you.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks who the weirdoes in the photo are and mentions an ugly kid
jokes.punchline: Vivian reveals it's the family themselves in their hippie phase
jokes.explanation: Classic family photo embarrassment joke with good setup/payoff

jokes.text: The bone in your hair is a good look for you, Hilary.
jokes.setup: Looking at embarrassing hippie-era family photo
jokes.punchline: Will comments on Hilary's ridiculous hippie styling
jokes.explanation: Visual gag referencing hippie fashion absurdity

jokes.text: Yeah, you've got sort of a 'yabba-dabba do.'
jokes.setup: Commenting on Hilary's bone hair accessory in old photo
jokes.punchline: References Flintstones caveman aesthetic
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that extends the visual gag

jokes.text: You have my legs, and the answer is no.
jokes.setup: Ashley wants to wear short skirts like her mom did, citing she has mom's legs
jokes.punchline: Phil says she has HIS legs, implying they're not as attractive
jokes.explanation: Good parental joke playing on genetics and Phil's self-awareness

jokes.text: I have known freedom, don't like the health plan.
jokes.setup: Marge encourages Geoffrey to be free from capitalist oppression
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey dryly notes the practical downsides of freedom
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey line combining dry wit with practical wisdom, very quotable

jokes.text: Whatever you say, Phil.
jokes.setup: Phil claims Geoffrey is more of a friend than a servant
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's response shows the obvious servant/master dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan that exposes the contradiction in Phil's statement

jokes.text: Cool, do you have any more?
jokes.setup: Teacher catches Will chewing gum, suggesting he's in trouble
jokes.punchline: Teacher asks for gum for himself instead of disciplining
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing the cool teacher's unconventional approach

jokes.text: And I'm gonna call this Midget at a Desk.
jokes.setup: Carlton presents his artistic shadow photograph titled 'Bunny on a Wall'
jokes.punchline: Teacher brutally renames it based on what it actually looks like
jokes.explanation: Harsh but funny reality check on Carlton's artistic pretensions

jokes.text: Mine ain't forged this time.
jokes.setup: Will asks about permission slips for field trip
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to previously forging permission slips
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's mischievous past

jokes.text: They thought The Little Mermaid was pornographic.
jokes.setup: Cornflake explains why his parents won't let him see naked photos
jokes.punchline: Reveals his parents' ridiculous prudishness about Disney movies
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation showing extreme parental prudishness

jokes.text: Turned me on.
jokes.setup: Discussion about The Little Mermaid being considered pornographic
jokes.punchline: Will's inappropriate admission
jokes.explanation: Cheap sex joke that relies on shock value rather than cleverness

jokes.text: I mean, I've spent the whole semester sucking up to you.
jokes.setup: Will protests the teacher being fired
jokes.punchline: Reveals his selfish motivation for caring
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's honest selfishness beneath his outrage

jokes.text: No, about how she used to always put her name on everything in the refrigerator.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Marge argued with Angela Davis about political views
jokes.punchline: Reveals petty roommate disputes instead of political differences
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that humanizes historical figures with mundane problems

jokes.text: Yeah, and I bet with that big old Afro, she probably had a lot of hair and Afro Sheen and stuff all over the place, right?
jokes.setup: Building on Angela Davis roommate complaints
jokes.punchline: Will imagines specific hair care product issues
jokes.explanation: Extends the roommate joke with specific cultural details

jokes.text: What, you just gonna dis me right in my face like that, Marge?
jokes.setup: Marge calls Will 'Hasani'
jokes.punchline: Will thinks it's an insult before learning it means handsome
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on Will's sensitivity and cultural ignorance

jokes.text: It's just 'Too Legit To Quit.'
jokes.setup: Will creates an African-sounding name for Marge
jokes.punchline: It's just MC Hammer lyrics
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that deflates the African naming convention

jokes.text: Ash, you kind of look like Winnie the Pooh Mandela.
jokes.setup: Ashley is dressed up in African-inspired activist outfit
jokes.punchline: Will combines Winnie the Pooh with Winnie Mandela
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay combining children's character with political figure

jokes.text: Come on, Daddy. Suppose Gandhi's parents treated him like this. Gandhi ate his peas.
jokes.setup: Ashley compares herself to Gandhi in protesting eating peas
jokes.punchline: Phil matter-of-factly states Gandhi ate his peas
jokes.explanation: Excellent parental comeback that deflates Ashley's grandiose comparison with absurd specificity

jokes.text: Could you put in a good word for me? I'm thinking of applying.
jokes.setup: Family discusses FBI wanting Marge
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks it's a job opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment showing his square, authority-loving nature

jokes.text: No way am I sharing a cell with a woman called Big Mama.
jokes.setup: Carlton realizes they're harboring a fugitive
jokes.punchline: His immediate concern is prison cellmate situations
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton panic moment with prison stereotype humor

jokes.text: Man, and I thought I was doing my part by going to Spike Lee movies.
jokes.setup: Marge describes her serious activist crimes
jokes.punchline: Will realizes his activism is just movie attendance
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about performative vs. real activism

jokes.text: Come on, Will. We're harboring a fugitive. Yeah. And ever since she got here my diary has been a lot more interesting.
jokes.setup: Carlton worries about harboring a fugitive
jokes.punchline: Ashley sees it as exciting diary material
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Carlton's anxiety and Ashley's excitement

jokes.text: I hate to sound paranoid... but do we really know if Mom and Dad are who they say they are? Or if their names are really Mom and Dad?
jokes.setup: Family discusses parents' radical past
jokes.punchline: Hilary questions if 'Mom' and 'Dad' are their real names
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary ditzy moment with unexpectedly philosophical questioning

jokes.text: 'Ricky Ricardo'? 'Lucy, you got some explaining to do.'
jokes.setup: Will signs petition with fake name
jokes.punchline: Carlton responds with I Love Lucy catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference callback to fake names theme

jokes.text: All right. My man Cornflake got the spirit. He's a little confused, but he's got the spirit.
jokes.setup: White student Cornflake enthusiastically joins Black Power chant
jokes.punchline: Will acknowledges the enthusiasm while noting the cultural disconnect
jokes.explanation: Good observation about well-meaning but misplaced solidarity

jokes.text: Will, I think I speak for all of us when I say... let's get the hell out of here.
jokes.setup: Carlton begins what sounds like a supportive speech
jokes.punchline: Reveals everyone wants to abandon the protest
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Carlton speaking for the group's cowardice

jokes.text: Where are the safety goggles? My butler forgot to pack them.
jokes.setup: Students trying to be radical protesters
jokes.punchline: Their privileged backgrounds show through safety concerns
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between radical posturing and privileged reality

jokes.text: This is so exciting. We're like The Mod Squad, without Julie.
jokes.setup: Students barricaded in classroom protesting
jokes.punchline: Carlton compares it to sanitized TV show
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton reference showing his TV-filtered view of rebellion

jokes.text: Man, you smell that? Damn, it's chimichanga day in the cafeteria.
jokes.setup: Tense protest moment in barricaded classroom
jokes.punchline: Will gets distracted by food smells, deflating the drama
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of serious protest moment with teenage priorities

jokes.text: We was kind of hoping the cheerleaders would overhear.
jokes.setup: Marge asks why they chanted 'Make love, not war'
jokes.punchline: Will admits their real motivation was impressing girls
jokes.explanation: Honest teenage motivation undermining political posturing

jokes.text: By the time you were 17, you burned your first bra. But I could go get one of Hilary's.
jokes.setup: Marge talks about what she did at 17
jokes.punchline: Will offers to get Hilary's bra to burn
jokes.explanation: Standard bra burning reference with sibling targeting

jokes.text: If you need me, I'll be upstairs saluting the flag.
jokes.setup: FBI agent arrives to investigate Marge
jokes.punchline: Carlton demonstrates his patriotic loyalty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment showing his authority worship

jokes.text: We tap every Black person's phone in the United States of America. Just a little FBI humor.
jokes.setup: Phil asks how FBI knows about Marge contacting them
jokes.punchline: Agent makes inappropriate joke about surveillance
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about real surveillance issues with uncomfortable truth

jokes.text: As a matter of fact, she said she was going to a nuclear power plant in Texas. But then again, she did mention a migrant workers' camp in Salinas. No, honey, she said she was going to a voters' registration drive in Biloxi. Well, I thought she was just going to Saks.
jokes.setup: FBI asks where Marge went
jokes.punchline: Family gives increasingly elaborate false information, ending with Hilary's shopping reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating misdirection ending with perfect Hilary non sequitur

jokes.text: Get the heck out of here. You all mean she ain't in the bathroom?
jokes.setup: Family gives elaborate false locations for Marge
jokes.punchline: Will innocently asks if she's just in the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of elaborate misdirection with simple question

jokes.text: Get the heck out of here. You all mean she ain't in the bathroom?
jokes.setup: Family giving various locations for Marge
jokes.punchline: Will's mundane assumption she's just in the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of elaborate misdirection with simple, obvious possibility. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: By the way, I don't think we've met. Ricky Ricardo.
jokes.setup: FBI agent is leaving
jokes.punchline: Will introduces himself with the fake name from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier fake name joke with perfect timing

jokes.text: 'You will become a famous rapper with your own TV show.' That's dope, right?
jokes.setup: Will reads his horoscope
jokes.punchline: Horoscope predicts exactly what Will Smith achieved in real life
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta joke about Will Smith's actual career trajectory, very clever fourth-wall break

jokes.text: 'You are headed for a fall. Beware. Accidents can happen.' Those things are so stupid.
jokes.setup: Hilary dismisses her ominous horoscope as ridiculous
jokes.punchline: Presumably falls or has an accident immediately after
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic timing joke with physical payoff

jokes.text: I wonder what I'll call my TV show?
jokes.setup: Following up on the horoscope about becoming a famous rapper with a TV show
jokes.punchline: Will wonders what to call the show (which is The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air)
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta callback extending the fourth-wall breaking joke about the show itself

tmdb_overview: Philip and Vivian welcome a '60s radical, who arouses the FBI's interest and influences Will and Carlton to take action over the firing of a nonconformist teacher.
